reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 2 (Trust Boundary) triggered. The vulnerability requires data from the Engine API (EL) to be malformed. According to BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json, the Engine API is SEMI_TRUSTED with JWT authentication and represents co-located EL-CL communication. The scope explicitly states that 'attacks requiring a compromised local EL may be considered conditional or lower severity.' The entry points listed in 01e (EngineAPI, Consensus) confirm this is reachable via Engine API. The REST API path (/eth/v2/beacon/blocks) is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE per conditional_scope.publicly_exposed_api. The code at beacon_validators.nim:589 shows proofs come from engineBlock.blobsBundle.proofs (Engine API), and rest_beacon_api.nim:1110 shows proofs come from REST API input. While the bounds check is indeed missing at peerdas_helpers.nim:328, exploiting this requires either (1) a compromised/buggy local EL sending malformed data, or (2) a publicly exposed REST API (out of scope). The 01e property PROP-57888860-pre-006 from file outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W3B4_1771748827.json states: 'Proof slicing indices in cells_and_kzg_proofs_construction must always be within blobs_bundle.proofs bounds' with entry_points=['EngineAPI', 'Consensus'], attacker_controlled=false, bug_bounty_scope='conditional'. This confirms the property itself acknowledges the conditional nature of this attack requiring Engine API compromise.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Proof slicing indices in cells_and_kzg_proofs_construction must always be within blobs_bundle.proofs bounds. (forall blob_i: start == i * CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB AND end == (i+1) * CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB AND end <= len(blobs_bundle.proofs))
